candidates.why: Explicit separate operating D&A row above EBIT, but nothing in the label ties it specifically to COGS / cost of sales relative to the selected COGS bucket 'Materials and Supplies'. Adding it to COGS would risk misclassification.
Separate operating D&A-like row above EBIT, but it is a broad total including impairment and is not identified as COGS-related. It should not be assigned to COGS without clearer evidence.

variables.notes: Selected COGS bucket is only 'Materials and Supplies'. The excerpt shows separate operating D&A lines, but none are labelled as belonging to COGS / cost of sales. Conservative treatment is to avoid adding any COGS D&A row separately.

candidates.why: Explicit separate operating depreciation line above EBIT and outside the selected XSGA component rows, so it could represent operating overhead D&A that is not embedded in those selected rows. However, the label does not indicate SG&A specifically, and it may span multiple functions.

Separate same-level operating D&A total above EBIT, which suggests D&A is not embedded in the selected XSGA rows. But it is a broad total that also includes impairment and may include non-SG&A portions, so assigning it to XSGA would risk overstatement and double counting.

variables.notes: The statement has separate operating D&A rows above EBIT, implying D&A may sit outside the selected XSGA buckets. But no row is clearly SG&A-specific, and the broader total includes impairment. To avoid double counting or functional misallocation, no XSGA_DA row is selected automatically.
